title: Earnings Call Transcript: Marqeta's Q1 2026 Results Beat Expectations With First GAAP Profit
text: Marqeta Q1 2026 Earnings: Key Discussion Points. Financial Performance: Marqeta achieved its first GAAP profitability in Q1 2026, reporting net income of $8 million with EPS of $0.02, significantly exceeding forecasts of -$0.0023. Revenue reached $166 million (19% YoY growth) while total payment volume surged 33% year-over-year to $112 billion. Key Growth Drivers: Product Expansion: The company highlighted momentum across multiple offerings. Buy Now, Pay Later grew nearly 60%, while expense management acceleration continued at over 40% growth rates. Management noted customers are often looking to serve several needs with a comprehensive offering spanning debit, credit, and secured solutions. International Scaling: Twelve of the top fifteen customers now operate across multiple countries on Marqeta's platform. Examples included Sezzle's Canadian virtual card launch and Ramp's expansion into Australia, Japan, Singapore, Brazil, and Mexico. New Customer Wins: The company secured a major customer launching a credit builder card and signed a personal finance assistant provider migrating existing portfolios to Marqeta's platform. Market Challenges: Block concentration declined to 42% of revenue as non-Block customers grew twice as fast. Executives acknowledged potential headwinds from Cash App new issuance delays and noted no notable shift in spend or consumer behavior, though macroeconomic risks remain. Forward Outlook: For 2026, management expects 12-14% net revenue growth and 10-12% gross profit growth, with adjusted EBITDA growth in the mid-to-high twenties range. The company projects approximately $15 million in full-year GAAP net income.
